description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, dark metallic head and a medium-brown wooden handle. The axe head is wedge-shaped with a single broad cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping tasks. The handle is slightly curved, ergonomically designed for a comfortable grip, and tapers gently towards the end. The wood grain is visible, giving the handle a natural, sturdy appearance, while the head is smoothly finished and securely mounted. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This axe would be commonly found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or park service areas for chopping wood or clearing brush.
description: A hand axe with a dark metal wedge-shaped head and a curved brown wooden handle, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The handle is ergonomic and the head is robust, with a single cutting edge.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top, showing the pointed end of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, clearly displaying the wedge-shaped blade and the full curvature of the handle.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the back, highlighting the flat poll of the head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: The axe is shown from the opposite side, again revealing the blade profile and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, ergonomic shape, rounded end, and oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll, no beard, and a smooth finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
